description: The order of knowing doesn't change what you know.

hints.text: The goal is $A \land B \to B \land A$. Conjunction is commutative — it doesn't care about order — but proving it requires both breaking apart and rebuilding. Start by assuming the left side.
You need both pieces individually before you can swap them. Extract $A$ with `and-elim-l`.
Now extract $B$ with `and-elim-r`. You now have both halves, just in the wrong order.
Use `and-intro` to put them back together — but this time put the step that gives $B$ first, and $A$ second.
